violations.text: Food was observed held at 43-48 degrees Fahrenheit in the prep unit next to the grill. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in the prep unit since the day prior. Food was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). The prep unit was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Prep unit must be repaired/replaced/adjusted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024

Cheese and chicken were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/12/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment must enroll employees in a food safety course. Please email inspector proof of enrollment. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023

There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. An employee illness policy was given to the person in charge during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023

The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Written procedures were given to the person in charge during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023

Raw animal foods (such as fish) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as tuna salad and dressing) in the prep unit. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as tuna salad and dressing during storage. Raw fish was relocated during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023

Food was observed held at 39-45 degrees Fahrenheit in the refrigerator. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All time/temperature control for safety foods were relocated to another refrigeration unit. Refrigeration unit was observed at an ambient air temperature of 45 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Establishment may only use the refrigerator for non time/temperature control for safety foods such as beverages. Per person in charge refrigeration services will be contacted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023

Multiple containers of food were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023

Chowder was observed held past 7 days in the 2-Door refrigerator in the shed. Per person in charge, chowder was made on 5/25/23. Chowder was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). . Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. A new sanitizing solution was made at 100 ppm during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023
The dipper well located in the ice cream cooler area was not in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of black debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023
